what: Human stature estimation from bone measures based on multiple formula
title: Human stature estimation from bone measures based on multiple formula
text: R package calculate human stature estimation bone measurements. statuAAR can calculate human stature estimations according several well established formula based measured bone length. need table individuals rows measurements columns, repeated individual ID one measurement per row. Different formulas can selected data set, e.g. Breitinge & Bach 1956, Pearson 1899 Ruff et al. 2012, correspondig results provided list one data frame formula. date, large number formulas developed various bone measurements reference populations, yielding wide range possible results. quality result depends mainly representativeness original sample used formula relation data collection hand. straightforward quick calculation using different formulas allows easy comparison different results. general introduction human stature estimation based bone measurements can found, example, Ruff, Chr. B. (ed.) (2018). Skeletal variation adaptation Europeans: upper Paleolithic Twentieth Century. Hoboken, NJ: John Wiley & Sons. Siegmund, F. (2019). Die Körpergröße der Menschen der Ur- und Frühgeschichte Mitteleuropas und ein Vergleich ihrer anthropologischen Schätzmethoden. (Beiträge zur Archäologie des Lebensstandards, 1). Norderstedt: BoD. provide description formula, including required possible bone measurements citations (s. bibliography file BibTeX). Bone measurements designated according : Martin, R. (1928). Lehrbuch der Anthropologie systematischer Darstellung: mit besonderer Berücksichtigung der anthropologischen Methoden / für Studierende, Ärzte und Forschungsreisende. 3 vols. Fischer: Jena. package still development, basic workflow data preparation, verification calculation possible. Explanatory vignettes preparation.

title: Human stature estimation from bone measures based on multiple formula
text: Get df available formula measures Check Breitinger & Bach 1965 documentation Read csv data, e.g. Bach1965.csv data.frame Prepare tabled data long list (statuaar_data_table) Check measurements using general statistics. Get stature estimations provided measurements. function returns list data frame formula. $bb65
code: statuAAR::getFormulaDataframe() ?statuAAR::breitinger_bach_1965 statuAAR::Bach1965 <- read.csv(     "data-raw/Bach1965.csv",     sep = ",",     header = TRUE,     skip=3,     stringsAsFactors = FALSE,     check.names = FALSE   ) dl.bach1965 <- statuAAR::prep.statuaar.data(Bach1965, d.form = "wide",                measures.names = "short", sex = "sex", stats = FALSE) statuAAR::measures.statistics(dl.bach1965) statuAAR::getStature(c('bb65'), dl.bach1965)

what: Calculate stature estimation according to: Boldsen 1984 — boldsen_1984
title: Calculate stature estimation according to: Boldsen 1984 — boldsen_1984
text: Stature estimation (mm) based mean regression calculations, separated  sex (Boldsen 1984). Bone measures used: Fem1, Tib1 bone measures left right provided mean value used, statistic information 2 bones counted (n_measures). sex indet. mean male female stature estimation given. skeleton series approximately 500 individuals medieval burial ground Lille Sct. Mikkelsgade Viborg, stature 65 well-preserved adult skeletons (31 male, 34 female) determined (Boldsen 1984, Appendix 1). Based stature estimates, four regression functions presented: separated gender Fem1 Tib1. Due almost identical slopes, functions aggregated according bone type separated intercept modern populations (Danes, Finns) White Americans data Trotter & Gleser (1958). , use formulas obtained primary archaeological data, results averaged bone measurements available. Returns data.frame : ind: individual identifyer (rownames), sex: provided calculation: m, f, indet. stature: estimated provided sex bone measures, bone (measure(s)): bones used calculation, if_female (stature): columns alternative stature three sex classes, if_male (stature), if_indet. (stature) n_measures: number bone measures included: e.g. 2 Fem2 (left, right) + 1 Tib1

title: Calculate stature estimation according to Breitinger 1938 & Bach 1965. — breitinger_bach_1965
text: Stature estimation (mm) based mean different regression calculations, separated  sex (Breitinger 1938, Bach 1965). Bone measures used: Hum2, Hum1, Rad1b, Fem1, Tib1b. bone measures left right provided mean value used, statistic information 2 bones counted (n_measures). sex indet. mean male female stature estimation given. Breitinger (1938) show regression equation Hum1, equation can derived values given table p. 272 (see Siegmund 2010, p. 112). contrast, Bach (1965) gives equations. addition, Bach (1965, 20) states use one Humeri measures estimation (Hum1 Hum2 mean ) avoid inadmissable multiplication bone within stature estimation. Returns data.frame : ind: individual identifyer (rownames), sex: provided calculation: m, f, indet. stature: estimated provided sex bone measures, bone (measure(s)): bones used calculation, if_female (stature): columns alternative stature three sex classes, if_male (stature), if_indet. (stature) n_measures: number bone measures included: e.g. 2 Fem2 (left, right) + 1 Tib1

title: Calculate stature estimation according to: Byers et al 1989. — byers_etal_1989
text: Stature estimation (mm) based regression calculation one Metatarsal 1 separated sex (Byers et al 1989). Bone measures used: Os metatarsale , apex capitulum midpoint articular surface base parallel longituninal axis bone. bone measures left right provided mean value used, statistic information 2 bones counted (n_measures). archaeological finds, group assignment (Euro-American Afro-American) possible. addition, gender-specific estimates MtI1 offer best accuracy. reason, following formulas used: Combined data (indet): stature = 634 + 16.8 (Metl), males: stature = 815 + 14.3 (Metl), females: stature = 783 + 13.9 (Metl). Returns data.frame : ind: individual identifyer (rownames), sex: provided calculation: m, f, indet. stature: estimated provided sex bone measures, bone (measure(s)): bones used calculation, if_female (stature): columns alternative stature three sex classes, if_male (stature), if_indet. (stature) n_measures: number bone measures included: e.g. 2 MtI1 (left, right)

code: # Read example dataset into a data frame x <- statuAAR::Feldesman1990 # Check for one individual per site table(table(x$Site)) #>  #>  1  #> 48   # Reduce gender, e.g. M(a), information to m, f. x$Probable.gender <- tolower(gsub("^(\\w).*", "\\1", x$Probable.gender)) x$Probable.gender[nchar(x$Probable.gender) == 0] <- "i"  # If not yet existent create a list of measure names to be used measures.concordance <- create.measures.concordance() # Edit the measures.concordance for this dataset measures.concordance$own[measures.concordance$short == "Fem1"] <- "Femur.length"  # Prepare tabled data into a long list (statuaar_data_table) dl.fe90 <- statuAAR::prep.statuaar.data(x, d.form = "wide", ind = "Site",               measures.names = "own", sex = "Probable.gender", stats = FALSE)  # Calculate stature estimation using this formula. fe90.estimates <- statuAAR::getStature(c("fe90"), dl.fe90)  # Extract the corresponding data frame from the returned list object. fe90.estimates[["fe90"]] #> [1] "There is no usable bone measurement / indice available for the chosen formula."

code: # Read example dataset into a data frame  # Read example dataset into a data frame x <- TrotterGleser1952 # If not yet existent create a list of measure names to be used # measures.concordance <- create.measures.concordance() # Edit the measures.concordance (not needed for this dataset) # measures.concordance$own[measures.concordance$short == "Fem1"] <- "Fem"  # get a dataframe with measures to process dl.trotter.gleser <- prep.statuaar.data(x, d.form = "wide",    ind = "Appendix_row", sex = "Sex", measures.names = "own", stats = FALSE) print(dl.trotter.gleser, n = 3) #> # A tibble: 920 × 4 #>   Ind   Sex   variable value #>   <chr> <fct> <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 1_3   m     Fem1       389 #> 2 2_2   m     Fem1       391 #> 3 1_4   m     Fem1       393 #> # ℹ 917 more rows  # See basic statistics to check for errors measures.statistics(dl.trotter.gleser) #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   measure     n  MinM Quart1 MedianM MeanM Quart3  MaxM #>   <chr>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Fem1      184   348    414     464   465    514   605 #> 2 Hum1      184   244    291     328   328    364   417 #> 3 Rad1      184   165    217     248   250    282   340 #> 4 Tib1      184   271    328     374   375    418   511 #> 5 Uln1      184   193    235     266   268    298   364  # For the data from Rollet 1888 rollet1888 <- Rollet1888 # 1. Create an identifyer due to identical numbering of females and males rollet1888$id <- paste(rollet1888$Sex, rollet1888$Nr, sep = "_") # 2. Fill in the mesasures names in the column "own" of the measures.concordance measures.concordance <- measures.concordance.rollet1888 measures.concordance[measures.concordance$own != "",] #>    short            long           own #> 1   Fem1         Femur.1           Fem #> 2  Fem1l    Femur.1.left    Femur.left #> 3  Fem1r   Femur.1.right   Femur.right #> 8  Fib1l   Fibula.1.left   Fibula.left #> 9  Fib1r  Fibula.1.right  Fibula.right #> 10  Hum1       Humerus.1           Hum #> 14 Hum1l  Humerus.1.left  Humerus.left #> 15 Hum1r Humerus.1.right Humerus.right #> 19  Rad1        Radius.1           Rad #> 23 Rad1l   Radius.1.left   Radius.left #> 24 Rad1r  Radius.1.right  Radius.right #> 28  Tib1         Tibia.1           Tib #> 35 Tib1l    Tibia.1.left    Tibia.left #> 36 Tib1r   Tibia.1.right   Tibia.right #> 37  Uln1          Ulna.1          Ulna #> 38 Uln1l     Ulna.1.left     Ulna.left #> 39 Uln1r    Ulna.1.right    Ulna.right  # 3. Transform data into a statuaar_data_table and basic statistics dl.rollet1888 <- prep.statuaar.data(rollet1888, d.form = "wide",       ind="id", sex = "Sex", measures.names = "own") #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   measure     n  MinM Quart1 MedianM MeanM Quart3  MaxM #>   <chr>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 NA          0    NA     NA      NA   NaN     NA    NA

title: Calculate stature estimation according to: Vercellotti et al. 2009 — vercellotti_etal_2009
text: Stature estimation (mm) based hierarchy different regression calculations, separated  sex developed 60 individuals medieval (XI-XII c) cemetery Giecz, Poland (Vercellotti et al. 2009). Bone measures used: Fem2+Tib1, Fem2, Fem1, Tib1, Hum1+Rad1, Hum1, Rad1 addition individual bone measurements performed calculation. bone measures left right provided mean value used, statistic information 2 bones counted (n_measures). Vercellotti et al. propose regression calculations combination male female individuals. regressions used case undetermined sex (indet.). Returns data.frame : ind: individual identifyer (rownames), sex: provided calculation: m, f, indet. stature: estimated provided sex bone measures, bone (measure(s)): bones used calculation, if_female (stature): columns alternative stature three sex classes, if_male (stature), if_indet. (stature) n_measures: number bone measures included: e.g. 2 Fem2 (left, right) + 1 Tib1
